title: Jedi Apprentice: The Only Witness
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}} {{Book |image=[[File:JA17Only_Witness.jpg]] |title=''Jedi Apprentice: The Only Witness'' |author=[[Jude Watson]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |cover artist=[[Cliff Nielsen]] |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[February]] [[2002]] |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages= |isbn=0 439 97922 6 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[40 BBY/Legends|40 BBY]] |series=''[[Star Wars: Jedi Apprentice]]'' |preceded by=[[Jedi Apprentice: The Call to Vengeance|''The Call to Vengeance'']] |followed by=[[Jedi Apprentice: The Threat Within|''The Threat Within'']] }} '''''Jedi Apprentice: The Only Witness''''' is the seventeenth in a series of young reader novels that chronicle the adventures of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] prior to [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. ==Publisher's summary==
'''Peace over Anger'''
'''Honor over Hate'''
'''Strength over Fear'''
''An evil crime family controls the fate of a planet. Only one witness can bring them down—someone from the inside. In order to make it off the planet to testify, she's going to need the protection of the Jedi''. ''Qui-Gon Jinn and Obi-Wan Kenobi take the assignment, thinking it will be easy. But nothing is ever easy when crime is concerned, and soon the two Jedi are entangled in a violent web of power, corruption, and lies. Qui-Gon and Obi-Wan know their allegiance is to the witness. But can she be trusted?'' ==Plot summary== One person holds the key to the downfall of a powerful [[The Cobral|criminal syndicate]]. She must be safely escorted offworld so that she can testify to the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|authorities]] just what she knows. [[Lena Cobral]] has agreed to testify against the rulers of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Frego]], the [[Cobral family|Cobral criminal family]]. [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan]] and [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon]] protect her as she gathers evidence and returns to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. As the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] discover, when dealing with powerful and corrupt [[Crime lord/Legends|criminals]], nothing can be taken for granted. ==Continuity== As an indication of [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|Episode II’s]] impending release, this [[2002]] novel featured an early appearance of [[Jedi librarian/Legends|Jedi librarian]], [[Jocasta Nu/Legends|Jocasta Nu]]. A scene set in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] mentioned [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Chancellor]] [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Valorum]], but term limitations prevented this from being the same chancellor seen in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|Episode I]] – Valorum did not become chancellor until [[39 BBY/Legends|39 BBY]], according to ''[[Cloak of Deception]]''. ==Media== ===Cover gallery=== This is a gallery of the different cover variations of ''Jedi Apprentice: The Only Witness''.
